  • Modules
  • Data Structures
  • Files
  • Examples

CPU Sets and Affinitization (tmc/cpus.h)
[Tilera Multicore Components (TMC&tm;) Library]

Detailed Description

Routines that manage sets of CPUs and control Linux processor affinity.

The Linux sched.h header provides a cpu_set_t object for representing sets of CPUs. The tmc/cpus.h header provides a variety of utility routines for manipulating these CPU sets, including:

  • Adding and removing CPUs and performing various set operations.
  • Binding tasks so that they only execute on a particular set of CPUs.
  • Converting cpu_set_t objects to array or string representations.
  • Converting a CPU's index number to and from its UDN coordinates.

Controlling a Task's CPU Affinity

The Linux scheduler allows applications to restrict the set of CPUs on which a particular task may execute. A "task" is defined as either a process (with no threads), or an individual thread within a threaded process. The tmc_cpus_get_my_affinity() call allows a task to determine its current CPU affinity. Similarly tmc_cpus_set_my_affinity() is used to set the task's affinity, binding it to a set of CPUs. Applications often need to bind tasks to a single CPU; the tmc_cpus_set_my_cpu() call provides a convenient wrapper for this use case.

Tilera recommends that developers avoid hard-coding particular CPU numbers in their applications. Instead, developers should use tile-monitor or taskset to pass an initial set of CPUs to an application, then have the application code select each task's CPU from the CPUs in that initial CPUs set. This approach makes it easier to modify the number of CPUs used for a particular application. It also avoids problems such as when the developer changes the set of tiles used for hypervisor dedicated tiles and the new locations conflict with CPU numbers hard-coded into an application.

Use tile-monitor's --tile option to control an application's initial CPU affinity. Alternatively, when running from the Linux console, use either the taskset command or the shepherd:

 shepherd --tile 4x1 <exe> <args>
 taskset -c 1-4 <exe> <args>

Running via taskset simply runs the program with the specified CPU affinity. The shepherd provides additional functionality, like automatic cleanup of application processes when any process exits due to a failure. For more information, see Task Management and Cleanup (tmc/task.h).

Once the application is running, it can use code like the following to assign each task to a CPU:

 // Save the application's CPU set for later use.
 cpu_set_t cpus;
 if (tmc_cpus_get_my_affinity(&cpus) != 0)
   tmc_task_die("tmc_cpus_get_my_affinity() failed.");

 // Given this task's rank, bind to the appropriate CPU.
 if (tmc_cpus_set_my_cpu(tmc_cpus_find_nth_cpu(&cpus, rank)) < 0)
   tmc_task_die("tmc_cpus_set_my_cpu() failed.");

 // Use the saved CPU set to look up UDN coordinates for rank 0.
 int rank0_cpu = tmc_cpus_find_nth_cpu(&cpus, 0);
 DynamicHeader header = tmc_udn_header_from_cpu(rank0_cpu);

Tilera Linux also provides a couple of special CPU sets. The online set, accessible via tmc_cpus_get_online_cpus(), includes all CPUs on which Linux tasks may run. The dataplane set, accessible via tmc_cpus_get_dataplane_cpus() or tile-monitor's --tile dataplane option, contains only those CPUs that were configured to run in dataplane mode as part of the Linux boot arguments. Dataplane CPUs are optimized for running a single task and attempt to avoid any Linux interrupt overhead so long as the task avoids making any Linux system calls.

The CPU Grid

The Tile Processor arranges CPUs into a two-dimensional grid. Thus, many applications, particularly those using the UDN, work with CPUs in terms of rectangular arrays, defined by a width and height, plus an upper left-hand (X,Y) CPU coordinate. TMC provides a variety of functions for determining the CPU grid size and manipulating CPU rectangles. For example, tmc_cpus_grid_tile_to_cpu() converts a CPU's (X,Y) coordinates to a CPU number.

Defines

#define TMC_CPUS_MAX_COUNT   __CPU_SETSIZE
 Maximum number of CPUs a cpu_set_t can represent.
#define TMC_CPUS_NUM_BITS   __NCPUBITS
 Number of bits per word in the __bits field of cpu_set_t.
#define TMC_CPUS_NUM_WORDS   (__CPU_SETSIZE / __NCPUBITS)
 Number of words in the __bits field of cpu_set_t.

Functions

void tmc_cpus_clear (cpu_set_t *s)
 Clear a CPU set.
int tmc_cpus_add_cpu (cpu_set_t *s, unsigned int cpu)
 Add a CPU to a CPU set.
int tmc_cpus_remove_cpu (cpu_set_t *s, unsigned int cpu)
 Remove a CPU from a CPU set.
void tmc_cpus_add_cpus (cpu_set_t *s, const cpu_set_t *cpus)
 Add some CPUs to a CPU set.
void tmc_cpus_remove_cpus (cpu_set_t *s, const cpu_set_t *cpus)
 Remove some CPUs from a CPU set.
void tmc_cpus_intersect_cpus (cpu_set_t *s, const cpu_set_t *cpus)
 Intersect some CPUs with a CPU set.
int tmc_cpus_has_cpu (const cpu_set_t *s, unsigned int cpu)
 Determine if a CPU is present in a CPU set.
unsigned int tmc_cpus_count (const cpu_set_t *s)
 Count the number of CPUs in a CPU set.
int tmc_cpus_find_nth_cpu (const cpu_set_t *s, unsigned int n)
 Find the nth CPU in a CPU set.
int tmc_cpus_find_first_cpu (const cpu_set_t *s)
 Find the first CPU in a CPU set.
int tmc_cpus_find_last_cpu (const cpu_set_t *s)
 Find the last CPU in a CPU set.
int tmc_cpus_to_array (const cpu_set_t *s, unsigned int *indices, unsigned int count)
 Convert a CPU set into a CPU array.
int tmc_cpus_from_array (cpu_set_t *s, const unsigned int *indices, unsigned int count)
 Convert a CPU array into a CPU set.
int tmc_cpus_to_string (const cpu_set_t *s, char *string, size_t limit)
 Convert a CPU set into a CPU string.
int tmc_cpus_from_string (cpu_set_t *s, const char *string)
 Convert a CPU string into a CPU set.
int tmc_cpus_set_task_affinity (const cpu_set_t *s, pid_t tid)
 Set the affinity mask of task tid to s.
int tmc_cpus_get_task_affinity (cpu_set_t *s, pid_t tid)
 Get the affinity mask of task tid into s.
int tmc_cpus_set_task_cpu (int cpu, pid_t tid)
 Bind task tid to a single CPU.
int tmc_cpus_get_task_cpu (pid_t tid)
 Get the single CPU to which task tid is bound.
int tmc_cpus_get_task_current_cpu (pid_t tid)
 Get the CPU on which task tid is currently running.
int tmc_cpus_set_my_affinity (const cpu_set_t *s)
 Set the affinity mask of the current task to s.
int tmc_cpus_get_my_affinity (cpu_set_t *s)
 Get the affinity mask of the current task into s.
int tmc_cpus_set_my_cpu (int cpu)
 Set the single CPU to which the current task is bound.
int tmc_cpus_get_my_cpu (void)
 Get the single CPU to which the current task is bound.
int tmc_cpus_get_my_current_cpu (void)
 Get the CPU on which the current task is currently running.
int tmc_cpus_set_dataplane (int flags) __asm__("set_dataplane")
 Request specific dataplane semantics for this task.
int tmc_cpus_get_online_cpus (cpu_set_t *s)
 Get the online CPUs.
int tmc_cpus_get_dataplane_cpus (cpu_set_t *s)
 Get the dataplane CPUs.
int tmc_cpus_grid_width (void)
 Get the width of the grid.
int tmc_cpus_grid_height (void)
 Get the height of the grid.
int tmc_cpus_grid_total (void)
 Get the total number of tiles in the grid.
int tmc_cpus_grid_contains_tile (unsigned int x, unsigned int y)
 Determine if the grid contains a specific tile.
int tmc_cpus_grid_contains_cpu (unsigned int cpu)
 Determine if the grid contains a specific CPU.
unsigned int tmc_cpus_grid_tile_to_cpu (unsigned int x, unsigned int y)
 Convert a tile (assumed to be contained by the grid) into a CPU.
void tmc_cpus_grid_cpu_to_tile (unsigned int cpu, unsigned int *x, unsigned int *y)
 Convert a CPU (assumed to be contained by the grid) into a tile.
void tmc_cpus_grid_add_all (cpu_set_t *s)
 Add all the CPUs in the grid to a CPU set.
int tmc_cpus_grid_add_rect (cpu_set_t *s, unsigned int x, unsigned int y, unsigned int w, unsigned int h)
 Add a rectangle of CPUs to a CPU set.
int tmc_cpus_grid_remove_rect (cpu_set_t *s, unsigned int x, unsigned int y, unsigned int w, unsigned int h)
 Remove a rectangle of CPUs from a CPU set.
int tmc_cpus_grid_bounding_rect (const cpu_set_t *s, unsigned int *xp, unsigned int *yp, unsigned int *wp, unsigned int *hp)
 Determine the bounding rectangle for a CPU set.

Define Documentation

#define TMC_CPUS_MAX_COUNT   __CPU_SETSIZE

Maximum number of CPUs a cpu_set_t can represent.

#define TMC_CPUS_NUM_BITS   __NCPUBITS

Number of bits per word in the __bits field of cpu_set_t.

#define TMC_CPUS_NUM_WORDS   (__CPU_SETSIZE / __NCPUBITS)

Number of words in the __bits field of cpu_set_t.

Function Documentation

void tmc_cpus_clear ( cpu_set_t *  s  ) 

Clear a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to be modified.
Examples:
tmc/multi_app/app.c.

int tmc_cpus_add_cpu ( cpu_set_t *  s,
unsigned int  cpu 
)

Add a CPU to a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to be modified.
cpu CPU number to be set in s.
Returns:
0 on success, -1 and set errno to EINVAL if CPU is illegal.
Examples:
tmc/multi_app/app.c.

int tmc_cpus_remove_cpu ( cpu_set_t *  s,
unsigned int  cpu 
)

Remove a CPU from a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to be modified.
cpu CPU number to be removed from s.
Returns:
0 on success, -1 and set errno to EINVAL if CPU is illegal.

void tmc_cpus_add_cpus ( cpu_set_t *  s,
const cpu_set_t *  cpus 
)

Add some CPUs to a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to be modified.
cpus The set of CPUs to be added to s.
Examples:
tmc/multi_app/app.c.

void tmc_cpus_remove_cpus ( cpu_set_t *  s,
const cpu_set_t *  cpus 
)

Remove some CPUs from a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to be modified.
cpus The set of CPUs to be removed from s.

void tmc_cpus_intersect_cpus ( cpu_set_t *  s,
const cpu_set_t *  cpus 
)

Intersect some CPUs with a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to be modified.
cpus The set of CPUs that should not be removed from s.

int tmc_cpus_has_cpu ( const cpu_set_t *  s,
unsigned int  cpu 
)

Determine if a CPU is present in a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set.
cpu CPU number to check.
Returns:
1 if cpu is present in s, else 0.
Examples:
mpipe/ingress/app.c.

unsigned int tmc_cpus_count ( const cpu_set_t *  s  ) 

Count the number of CPUs in a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set.
Returns:
The number of CPUs present in s.
Examples:
mica/queue/app.c, mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, mpipe/loopback/app.c, mpipe/multi_rules/app.c, tmc/memory_perf/memory.c, tmc/multi_app/app.c, tmc/multi_tile/multi_tile.c, tmc/queue/bench_one_to_one.c, and trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c.

int tmc_cpus_find_nth_cpu ( const cpu_set_t *  s,
unsigned int  n 
)

Find the nth CPU in a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set.
n index (starting with 0).
Returns:
The CPU number of the nth CPU present in s. -1 if s does not have at least n+1 CPUs present.
Examples:
mica/queue/app.c, mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, mpipe/loopback/app.c, mpipe/multi_rules/app.c, tmc/memory_perf/memory.c, tmc/multi_app/app.c, tmc/multi_tile/multi_tile.c, tmc/queue/bench_one_to_one.c, trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c, trio/gxpci_host_pq/host.c, and trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c.

int tmc_cpus_find_first_cpu ( const cpu_set_t *  s  ) 

Find the first CPU in a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set.
Returns:
The CPU number of the first CPU present in s. -1 if s does not contain any CPUs.
Examples:
mica/comp/app.c, mica/crypto/app.c, mica/dma/app.c, and mpipe/ingress/app.c.

int tmc_cpus_find_last_cpu ( const cpu_set_t *  s  ) 

Find the last CPU in a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set.
Returns:
The CPU number of the last CPU present in s. -1 if s does not contain any CPUs.

int tmc_cpus_to_array ( const cpu_set_t *  s,
unsigned int *  indices,
unsigned int  count 
)

Convert a CPU set into a CPU array.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to be converted.
indices An array of integers to be filled with the CPU numbers that are present in s.
count The number of integers in the indices array.
Returns:
-1 if the vector was not large enough, else the number of indices used.

int tmc_cpus_from_array ( cpu_set_t *  s,
const unsigned int *  indices,
unsigned int  count 
)

Convert a CPU array into a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to be filled.
indices An array of CPU numbers to be set in s.
count The number of integers in the indices array.
Returns:
-1 if any CPU is illegal (not less than TMC_CPUS_MAX_COUNT), else zero.

int tmc_cpus_to_string ( const cpu_set_t *  s,
char *  string,
size_t  limit 
)

Convert a CPU set into a CPU string.

A CPU string has the form "A,B,C-F", where A, B, and C through F inclusive are the CPU numbers present in the CPU set. This routine currently only generates a comma-separated cpu list.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to be converted.
string The string to be filled.
limit The maximum number of characters in string.
Returns:
-1 if the string was not large enough, else the number of characters used (not including the final NUL terminator).

int tmc_cpus_from_string ( cpu_set_t *  s,
const char *  string 
)

Convert a CPU string into a CPU set.

A CPU string has the form "A,B,C-F", where A, B, and C through F inclusive are the CPU numbers present in the CPU set. For backwards compatibility, we accept ";" as a synonym for ",".

Parameters:
s The CPU set to be filled.
string A CPU string containing the CPUs to be set in s.
Returns:
-1 if the string was formatted incorrectly or any CPU is illegal (negative or not less than TMC_CPUS_MAX_COUNT), else zero.

int tmc_cpus_set_task_affinity ( const cpu_set_t *  s,
pid_t  tid 
)

Set the affinity mask of task tid to s.

Parameters:
s The new task affinity.
tid The task ID whose affinity is set. Use tid zero to act like tmc_cpus_set_my_affinity().
Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else 0.

int tmc_cpus_get_task_affinity ( cpu_set_t *  s,
pid_t  tid 
)

Get the affinity mask of task tid into s.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to be filled with the affinity of task tid.
tid The task ID whose affinity is read. Use tid zero to act like tmc_cpus_get_my_affinity().
Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else 0.

int tmc_cpus_set_task_cpu ( int  cpu,
pid_t  tid 
)

Bind task tid to a single CPU.

Parameters:
cpu The CPU number on which tid should run.
tid The task ID of the task to be affinitized. Use tid zero to act like tmc_cpus_set_my_cpu().
Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else 0.

int tmc_cpus_get_task_cpu ( pid_t  tid  ) 

Get the single CPU to which task tid is bound.

Note that this call will return -1 unless the current task is bound with an affinity of a single CPU. This can be useful to confirm that the task will not migrate among multiple CPUs. See also tmc_cpus_get_task_current_cpu().

Parameters:
tid The task ID of the task whose affinity is read. Use tid zero to act like tmc_cpus_get_my_cpu().
Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else a CPU number. If the task is not bound to a single CPU, errno is set to ERANGE.

int tmc_cpus_get_task_current_cpu ( pid_t  tid  ) 

Get the CPU on which task tid is currently running.

See also tmc_cpus_get_task_cpu().

CAUTION: if the given task is not bound to a single CPU, it may migrate to another CPU at any time.

Parameters:
tid The task ID of the task whose affinity is read. Use tid zero to act like tmc_cpus_get_my_current_cpu().
Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else a CPU number.

int tmc_cpus_set_my_affinity ( const cpu_set_t *  s  ) 

Set the affinity mask of the current task to s.

Parameters:
s The new task affinity.
Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else 0.
Examples:
tmc/multi_app/app.c.

int tmc_cpus_get_my_affinity ( cpu_set_t *  s  ) 

Get the affinity mask of the current task into s.

Parameters:
s The CPU set filled with the current task's affinity.
Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else 0.
Examples:
mica/comp/app.c, mica/crypto/app.c, mica/dma/app.c, mica/queue/app.c, mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/ingress/app.c, mpipe/loopback/app.c, mpipe/multi_rules/app.c, tmc/memory_perf/memory.c, tmc/multi_app/app.c, tmc/multi_tile/multi_tile.c, tmc/queue/bench_one_to_one.c, trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c, trio/gxpci_host_pq/host.c, trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and usb_dev/xfer/ud.c.

int tmc_cpus_set_my_cpu ( int  cpu  ) 

Set the single CPU to which the current task is bound.

Parameters:
cpu The CPU number of the single CPU on which the current task should run.
Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else 0.
Examples:
mica/comp/app.c, mica/crypto/app.c, mica/dma/app.c, mica/queue/app.c, mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, mpipe/ingress/app.c, mpipe/loopback/app.c, mpipe/multi_rules/app.c, tmc/memory_perf/memory.c, tmc/multi_app/app.c, tmc/multi_tile/multi_tile.c, tmc/queue/bench_one_to_one.c, trio/gxpci_c2c/c2c_send.c, trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c, trio/gxpci_host_pq/host.c, trio/gxpci_mmi/mmi.c, and usb_dev/xfer/ud.c.

int tmc_cpus_get_my_cpu ( void   ) 

Get the single CPU to which the current task is bound.

Note that this call will return -1 unless the current task is bound with an affinity of a single CPU. This can be useful to confirm that the task will not migrate among multiple CPUs. See also tmc_cpus_get_my_current_cpu().

Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else a CPU number. If the task is not bound to a single CPU, errno is set to ERANGE.

int tmc_cpus_get_my_current_cpu ( void   ) 

Get the CPU on which the current task is currently running.

This is the same as the glibc sched_getcpu() function (only available when -D_GNU_SOURCE or equivalent is specified).

This is faster than tmc_cpus_get_my_cpu().

CAUTION: If the current task is not bound to a single CPU, it may migrate to another CPU at any time.

Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else a CPU number.
Examples:
trio/gxpci_host_nic/gxpci_echo_drv_module.c, and usb_dev/xfer/ud.c.

int tmc_cpus_set_dataplane ( int  flags  ) 

Request specific dataplane semantics for this task.

The various flags allow a task to minimize kernel interrupts, or to diagnose userspace or kernel issues that cause interrupts. Note that you will also need to include <sys/dataplane.h> to get the flag names to use for the bitwise OR for the "flags" parameter, as this call is just a wrapper for the set_dataplane(2) system call. The flag values are:

DP_QUIESCE Quiesce the timer interrupt before returning to user space after a system call. Normally if a task on a dataplane core makes a syscall, the system will run one or more timer ticks after the syscall has completed, causing unexpected interrupts in userspace. Setting DP_QUIESCE avoids that problem by having the kernel "hold" the task in kernel mode until the timer ticks are complete. This will make syscalls dramatically slower. This flag also makes the core release any free kernel pages it is holding, so that it won't later need to do housekeeping to release them (for example, if mlockall() is called on another core). If multiple dataplane tasks are scheduled on a single core, neither one will make progress, since for the kernel to run both would require setting up a scheduler timer tick to allow the two tasks to multitask. Accordingly, both stay in the kernel until one is killed manually. This technique allows short-lived kernel tasks which run in response to syscalls to do their work and return to their normal idle state synchronously, allowing the dataplane task to continue in userspace afterwards without further interruption.

DP_STRICT Disallow the application from entering the kernel in any way, unless it calls set_dataplane() again without this bit set. Issuing any other syscall or causing a page fault would generate a kernel message, and "kill -9" the process. Setting this flag automatically sets DP_QUIESCE as well, to hold the process in kernel space until any timer ticks due to the set_dataplane() call have completed.

DP_DEBUG Debug dataplane interrupts, so that if any interrupt source attempts to involve a dataplane cpu, a kernel message and stack backtrace will be generated on the console. As this warning is a slow event, it may make sense to avoid this mode in production code to avoid making any possible interrupts even more heavyweight. Setting this flag automatically sets DP_QUIESCE as well.

Parameters:
flags the specific semantics being requested
Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else 0.

int tmc_cpus_get_online_cpus ( cpu_set_t *  s  ) 

Get the online CPUs.

The online CPU set includes all CPUs on which Linux tasks can run. Thus, it excludes CPUs that are dedicated for use by the hypervisor or bare metal environments.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to be filled.
Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else 0.

int tmc_cpus_get_dataplane_cpus ( cpu_set_t *  s  ) 

Get the dataplane CPUs.

Dataplane CPUs use Linux kernel code that minimizes the number of system interrupts in cases where a single task is homed on a CPU. The dataplane CPU set is configured via the "dataplane=N-M,..." boot argument passed to the Linux kernel.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to be filled.
Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else 0.
Examples:
mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, and mpipe/ingress/app.c.

int tmc_cpus_grid_width ( void   ) 

Get the width of the grid.

Returns:
The number of tiles in the X dimension of the Linux CPU grid, or -1 on failure (setting errno).

int tmc_cpus_grid_height ( void   ) 

Get the height of the grid.

Returns:
The number of tiles in the Y dimension of the Linux CPU grid, or -1 on failure (setting errno).

int tmc_cpus_grid_total ( void   ) 

Get the total number of tiles in the grid.

Returns:
The total number of tiles in the Linux CPU grid, or -1 on failure (setting errno).

int tmc_cpus_grid_contains_tile ( unsigned int  x,
unsigned int  y 
)

Determine if the grid contains a specific tile.

This function verifies that the specified tile coordinate is legal, and does not check if the tile is actually online.

Parameters:
x The tile's X coordinate.
y The tile's Y coordinate.
Returns:
1 if the Linux CPU grid contains the specified tile, else 0.

int tmc_cpus_grid_contains_cpu ( unsigned int  cpu  ) 

Determine if the grid contains a specific CPU.

This function verifies that the specified CPU number is legal, and does not check if the CPU is actually online.

Parameters:
cpu A CPU number.
Returns:
1 if the Linux CPU grid contains the specified CPU, else 0.

unsigned int tmc_cpus_grid_tile_to_cpu ( unsigned int  x,
unsigned int  y 
)

Convert a tile (assumed to be contained by the grid) into a CPU.

Parameters:
x The tile's X coordinate.
y The tile's Y coordinate.
Returns:
The CPU number corresponding to the provided tile coordinates.

void tmc_cpus_grid_cpu_to_tile ( unsigned int  cpu,
unsigned int *  x,
unsigned int *  y 
)

Convert a CPU (assumed to be contained by the grid) into a tile.

Parameters:
cpu A CPU number
x Filled with the X coordinate for cpu.
y Filled with the Y coordinate for cpu.

void tmc_cpus_grid_add_all ( cpu_set_t *  s  ) 

Add all the CPUs in the grid to a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to which CPUs are added.

int tmc_cpus_grid_add_rect ( cpu_set_t *  s,
unsigned int  x,
unsigned int  y,
unsigned int  w,
unsigned int  h 
)

Add a rectangle of CPUs to a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set to which CPUs are added.
x Minimum X coordinate of CPU rectangle.
y Minimum Y coordinate of CPU rectangle.
w Width (x dimension) of CPU rectangle.
h Height (y dimension) of CPU rectangle.
Returns:
0 on success. On failure, returns -1 and sets errno to EINVAL if the provided coordinates do not fit in the Linux CPU grid.

int tmc_cpus_grid_remove_rect ( cpu_set_t *  s,
unsigned int  x,
unsigned int  y,
unsigned int  w,
unsigned int  h 
)

Remove a rectangle of CPUs from a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set from which CPUs are removed.
x Minimum X coordinate of CPU rectangle.
y Minimum Y coordinate of CPU rectangle.
w Width (x dimension) of CPU rectangle.
h Height (y dimension) of CPU rectangle.
Returns:
0 on success. On failure, returns -1 and sets errno to EINVAL if the provided coordinates do not fit in the Linux CPU grid.

int tmc_cpus_grid_bounding_rect ( const cpu_set_t *  s,
unsigned int *  xp,
unsigned int *  yp,
unsigned int *  wp,
unsigned int *  hp 
)

Determine the bounding rectangle for a CPU set.

Parameters:
s The CPU set.
xp The X coordinate of the resulting rectangle.
yp The Y coordinate of the resulting rectangle.
wp The width of the resulting rectangle.
hp The height of the resulting rectangle.
Returns:
-1 on failure (setting errno), else 0.
